759.name: Into the Swamplands
759.description: So much for avoiding the swampier terrain. You rapidly plunge into a warm mist and your footing becomes more uncertain with a combination of poor visibility and small sinkholes of slimy water. You could continue on, but who knows what direction to go? The swamp extends to your northwest and southwest around a large tree. Escape lies southeast.

760.name: In the Swamp
760.description: The ground turns more swampy the further south you go. Eventually you imagine you'll be waist deep in the ol' bayou. Noises echo through the ancient trees here and you stop to wonder exactly how prepared you are. Northeast will take you out of here, anywhere else heads deeper into the swamp.

761.name: In the Swamp
761.description: A wide, hexagonal hole can be seen here underneath an arching root of the enormous tree to your east; a tell tale sign of burrowing hornets. Knowing never to venture down one, you wonder if stomping would get some attention. Except for the tree blocking your path east, the swamp continues unabated.

762.name: In the Swamp
762.description: Your feet squish deep into the moss. Indiscernible noises echo from deep within the swamp. You find yourself wishing for a bath and a set of clean clothes. The smooth flow of the river is audible to the west and a massive hardwood reaches to the heavens to your northeast. North or east will lead you around its base.

npcs.name: Grimy quadruped
npcs.description: with caked mud.  A long flat lower jaw perpetually chews dark green-brown

763.name: In the Swamp
763.description: Dull colored fungi are attached across the multitude of roots weaving through the swamp. Tiny patches of a brighter fungi can be seen around the bases of the enormous tree towering north of you. Heading west or northeast will trace you around its gargantuan base. More mud, more swamp, more bugs lie to southwest and south.

764.name: In the Swamp
764.description: The ground swallows your steps almost up to your knee. A rotted and gnarled tree blocks the north and west, while indiscernible noises echo from deeper south in the swamp. Some grey stone can be seen to your east though the muck impedes your travel that way. You find yourself wishing for a bath and a set of clean clothes.

765.name: In the Swamp
765.description: A thin rivulet flows inland from the calm bayou directly on your east. As it heads west, it fades into what must be a huge pool of quickmud. The toss of a rock confirms this and you look around for ways around it. Arching out from the center of the treacherous pool reaches the enormous root of an unseen tree. To the southwest a series flat stones provide another path.

se.to_room_name: At the Base of the Tree

767.name: In the Swamp
767.description: A featureless stone cylinder protrudes out from the swamp floor. Stepping east, the ground swallows your steps almost up to your knee; it will be futile to attempt to travel any further that way. Either northeast around the odd stone structure, or south and west toward more swampland.

npcs.name: Raven
npcs.description: straying for more than a brief moment.  Its jet pinfeathers ruffle slightly

768.name: In the Swamp
768.description: The air is thick and muggy and almost difficult to inhale. Insects land on your skin for a moment to catch their breath and buzz off again. A hazardous mix of deep swampy quicksand and thickly tangled bramble means travel east or south will be impossible.

769.name: In the Swamp
769.description: You trudge through the muggy swamp seeing few signs of life, but feeling lots of them. Long slimy vines hang low across all paths blocking your view in every direction. East, southeast, southwest, north -- it all looks the same from here. You wonder if you will ever again see home.

npcs.name: Some bindweed
npcs.description: Some of it clings uselessly to your legs while more of it winds towards

770.name: In the Swamp
770.description: Odd, the sound of flowing water can be heard from what sounds vaguely like directly underneath you. A wall of thorns and brambles halts any progress southward. A massive tree can be seen to a ways to the north. The raven flies northeast.

771.name: In the Swamp
771.description: Various bushes are growing out of the swampy earth. Tracing the roots of one plant deep into the earth ends abruptly as you find your arm completely submerged in the mud. The bayou can be heard just to your west, but the swamp floor would quickly swallow you and your possessions whole. You can head northeast or east from here.

npcs.name: Snapping turtle
npcs.description: attack people.  Judging by the gnarled skin and misshapen shell, it looks like this particular one has been in its fair share of fights too, and won.

772.name: The Bayou
772.description: Murky water bends lazily from west to south, a thorny thicket collecting on the north shore preventing landing there. O'erhanging ferny trees drip condensed moisture like a persistent warm rain.

773.name: The Bayou
773.description: The banks of the river broaden and melt into an unlandable mossy bog. The waterway ahead is certainly not fit for trade, nor the surrounding land suitable for cultivation. You get the feeling few come this far willingly.

776.name: The Bayou
776.description: The trees thicken and broaden their reach, the wide river flowing through a steamy tunnel of growth. A muddy bank is visible between the trunks to the north and a narrow cleft in thicker growth leads to the southwest.

777.name: In the Swamp
777.description: Swampground made moist by the nearby bayou sucks happily at your footwear as you trudge through it. The ruins of a house can be seen to the southwest and the swamp continues on to the west. A break in the bushes to the northeast leads to the flowing water.

779.name: In the Swamp
779.description: A path of stone slabs provide passage through a particularly treacherous area of the swamp. Just off to one side of the path is an enormous stone well sunk deep into the quickmud. Judging by the stench and ugly noises emanating from its depths, you'll want some quick reflexes were you to venture in. The stone path leads northeast or southeast away from the well.

784.name: In the Swamp
784.description: You stand pensively atop the very tip of a submerged tree. The only evidence of its existence are its enormous roots reaching like the legs of a spider to the edges of the quickmud. The only safe routes seem to be northwest toward some sort of cottage, toward the bayou to the east or north across the biggest root you've ever seen.

791.name: The Bayou
791.description: You continue to be slowly soaked by the muggy air and the drizzle from the broad leaves above as you float along the river. The bank is still reachable to the east and a narrow tributary flows unsurely from the west.

792.name: The Bayou
792.description: The river broadens and deepens for a short space here, the water nearly coming to a halt. A bank off to the east may go somewhere, but you dare not push westward into a hazily visible quickmud bog.

793.name: The Bayou
793.description: Steam rises from the bayou river and disappears, absorbed by the muggy swamp air. Dipping in a hand confirms that the water beneath you is indeed above body temperature. The simmering river could well be the reason for swampy conditions this far inland. Banks to either side remain landable. A patch of vibrant green watercress grows here, its leaves crisp and fresh.

npcs.name: Black crocodile
npcs.description: Its enormous size and girth fortunately make it a bit slower than you, and

795.name: Crazy River
795.description: Everything is bathed is a misty twilight, and sound is muffled by the warm fog that coils just above land. The river itself is utterly silent, the only ambient sound coming in the form of strange calls far in the distance. Threatening looking vines impose upon you from both banks, rustling occasionally with the passing of some unseen predator.

796.name: A Bad Place
796.description: There is something wrong here, an almost tangible presence in the air letting you know that you are not welcome. Frightening totems dot the banks and hang from branches over the river, silently warning you that nothing good lies ahead. Perhaps you should just turn back now.

797.name: Log Jam
797.description: Thick vegetation chokes the slow moving river here, snagging anything that the current drags this way. Jagged spars of wood jut above the surface, and you imagine there are plenty more deadheads floating below. The jam appears to be holding back quite a bit of water, not to mention your progress. You'll have to find another way 'round.

798.name: In the Swamp
798.description: You get the feeling that the vines to the south would not even yield to the sturdiest of axes. The trees have become too thick to the north to make any kind of real progress. You involuntarily wrinkle your nose up in stout defiance and tighten your tired muscles as you prepare to travel westward.

799.name: In the Swamp
799.description: A tremendous shelf of rock towers into the sky. Scattered about the ground are tufts of hair and various animal bones, the signs of a carnivorous creature's meals. The jungle is less oppressive here, and you visibly relax and begin to take deep breaths, relieved that you are still in one piece.

go shelf.to_zone: Sskyth Treetop Village - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Tan cougar
npcs.description: ears, powerful jaws, long, pointed canine teeth, and sharp, retractable claws on each paw.  The cougar is a carnivore.

815.name: At the Base of the Tree
815.description: You slosh loudly through the shallow water, which is choked with weeds and swamp plants. The sound of the river is now so distant you have to strain to hear it, the stagnant swamp a dim reflection of the flowing waters. Before you is a truly gigantic tree, which is so high the trunk is concealed by the thick canopy above. You notice a curious set of notches in the wood.

816.name: Murky Shore
816.description: The stagnant lake that adjoins the River Frektal ends here, becoming shallower and shallower as the river bed rises to a bank of thick, dark mud. Scattered reeds and bushes grow out into the water, and you almost trip on a broken wooden stump - though it isn't a stump at all. On closer inspection, you see lines of these broken posts, half-rotten by the water, that must have once supported a jetty of some kind.

e.to_room_name: At the Base of the Tree

817.name: Stagnant Water
817.description: This circular body of water, formed as the river Frektal pours out of its main course into the virgin swamp, is almost completely still, a strange shade of murky brown. The occasional ripple hints at unknown creatures swimming deep underwater, but otherwise the only signs of life are the reeds that surround the lake.

844.name: Deep in the Swamp
844.description: The rocky bluffs to your east decline sharply here, tumbling down into pits of quickmud on either side of the somewhat solid path upon which you tread. Far to the east you hear the distant bubbling of the Frektal, but the reeds and trees are too high and thick to make out much of anything westwards.

845.name: Deep in the Swamp
845.description: You hop from patch to patch of spongy, yet for the most part solid, ground between gnarled trees and wide pools of slurping quickmud. There is less surface water in this part of the swamp, but the springy ground and abundant quickmud tell you it's not far below your feet.

npcs.name: Pocket horse
npcs.description: is more than capable of defending itself when threatened.

846.name: Deep in the Swamp
846.description: The trees here are different from those in the heart of the swamp. Twisting root systems rise above your head, so as to benefit from the seasonal floods that cover this area. For most of the year however, the water is below ground, creating treacherous sink holes like those to your south and east. Tall stalks of hemlock rise from damp ground, white flowers deceptively delicate.

847.name: Deep in the Swamp
847.description: Tall brown grasses and dark green reeds sprout up from the peat floor of the swamp. In places the water table is so close below your feet that the ground bounces as though on springs. This is beneficial to the larger trees; like the one to your north, so heavy that it creates a depression, constantly filled with water in which to soak upraised roots.

848.name: Deep in the Swamp
848.description: At one step the ground can be solid enough to support your weight, and with the next - You're knee deep in foul smelling mire. The whole area stinks enough as it is, but something about disturbing the mud or water lets out a noxious reek above and beyond the regular, persistent odor. The land appears to rise a little to the south.

npcs.name: Some primordial ooze
npcs.description: acids moves its way through the swamp by some unknown means of locomotion, consuming anything small enough to get caught up inside.

849.name: Deep in the Swamp
849.description: Deep quagmires block passage westward, and to the north a massive hardwood looms out of a thicket of tall, upraised roots. Passages through the reeds skirt the quickmud pits, staying as much as possible to the semi-solid ground of the fen. Dark iron streaks run thick through the rock.

850.name: Deep in the Swamp
850.description: The root systems of two imposing trees flanking you meet in the center of the path and twine together, forcing you to squirm your way past. Large puddles of water are collected at the base of each, into which tiny amphibian forms dart for cover, swimming out of sight into the bracken at the bottom.

npcs.name: Grotesque rodent
npcs.description: shorter face and no tail.  It has folding hind legs like a hare, and sports

851.name: Deep in the Swamp
851.description: A particularly large tree is surrounded my a small pond just to your southwest, and hundreds of its long, vinelike roots pepper the water. Tall reeds with stiff wedge shaped leaves grow all around both the edge of it, and the large pits of quickmud to the north. Solid ground leads off east or south.

852.name: Deep in the Swamp
852.description: A massive weeping willow looms over this patch of land like a king, creating a small clearing within its curtain of overhanging branches. The ground is littered with the tree's thin golden leaves, and little can be seen outside the confines of its tiny domain.

853.name: Deep in the Swamp
853.description: The faint trickle of water can be heard somewhere off to your west, possibly the Frektal, but direction and distance become so distorted within the tall roots and reeds that you cannot be certain. A huge weeping willow tree, crowned in gold, dominates a clearing to the south.

854.name: Deep in the Swamp
854.description: The sound of running water, off to the northwest, is obvious now, and seems close enough to be within sight, were your view not obscured by the thick reeds and grasses. Still, the amount of quickmud just southwest of you tells you that water cannot be far off. A gnarled plant with twisted roots grows here, its leaves dark and waxy.

855.name: Deep in the Swamp
855.description: Just to the south, the ground gives way into sloppy muck then to a large pit of quickmud. Thick brambles choke the boggy ground to your north, though you could skirt their southern edge, passing westard by the quickmud as well. The way east is clear, save for tall swaying grasses.

856.name: Deep in the Swamp
856.description: A wide pool, its surface choked in scum, spreads out northwards; likely a remnant of last years floods. The water looks stagnant, and even less appetizing than the other ponds youve come across thus far. Other than several dead, gray trees, nothing grows from the mire save the disgusting yellow algae.

857.name: Deep in the Swamp
857.description: Wide swaths have been pushed through the brown grasses from the passing of some large beast, making convenient trails, though they don't seem to lead in any specific direction. That's what you get for following around a dumb grazing animal.

858.name: Deep in the Swamp
858.description: Strange cries are carried in on the smelly breeze, mingling with the sounds of the rustling grasses and the ever present sucking noise every time you pull your foot from the clinging mud. It sticks to your boots and clothes, fortunately the swamp smells just as bad as you so nobody can notice. A gnarled plant with twisted roots grows here, its leaves dark and waxy.

859.name: Deep in the Swamp
859.description: The swamp seems to go on forever, endless trails through the tall grasses and tangled roots snaking past wide quagmires and stagnant pools that breed all manner of putrid scum. Slimy creatures of indeterminate species plunge into the filthy water to hide from your lumbering presence.

860.name: Deep in the Swamp
860.description: Small pits of quickmud pepper the area, not big enough to be a real danger, but certainly more than an inconvenience if you were to be foolish enough to step into one, or just unlucky. Either way, watching ones step carefully in a place like this is would be fairly pragmatic.

861.name: Deep in the Swamp
861.description: Wiry roots reach upwards like stiff little fingers around several sapling trees which compete for space and light with the sharp green reeds. Gnats and noseeums hum annoyingly in your ears, their itching bites tormenting exposed skin. A foul stench rises from the ground every time you disturb the muck

862.name: Deep in the Swamp
862.description: All around you the massive swamp stretches as far as the eye can see; which really isn't all that far, as gangly trees and thick brown grasses block most of your view. A soupy looking patch of quickmud to the southwest has an odd red tinge to it, and a thick log spans the pit.

863.name: Deep in the Swamp
863.description: The upraised roots of a half dozen tall tress form an impenetrable thicket to the south, blocking off passage to any but the tiniest of creatures. Northwards, the swamp stretches off in all directions, paths through the reeds and grasses clearly visible, though not their destinations.

npcs.name: Ball of lightning
npcs.description: erratically flitting through the Great Ancestral swamp.  Many have tried to explain their existence, but none can be certain.

864.name: Deep in the Swamp
864.description: All around you green reeds and tall grasses sway upon a rank breeze, reaching your nostrils with wispy fingers and nearly bringing up your last meal. Here and there a tall tree, or a shattered deadhead juts above the sea of green and brown, but for the most part little can be seen at any distance.

865.name: Deep in the Swamp
865.description: Trying to find your way through the twisting paths between the pits of quickmud and thickets of twisting roots is more of a chore than you had expected. Trying to do so safely is nigh on impossible. Add in the swarms of biting flies, and numerous unseen predators lurking in the reeds and, well, just look at the mess you've got yourself in.

866.name: Deep in the Swamp
866.description: Stiff leaved reeds sprout up all around you, except to the east, where a wide pit of quickmud awaits its next victim. Here and there, the reeds have been spread apart by the passing of animals canny enough to avoid the deadly sink holes. It isn't clear from here, but it appears there may be a rise in the land off to the southwest.

867.name: Deep in the Swamp
867.description: A small hill rises out of the swamp to your east, crowned with a tuft of tall dead grass. Grinning like the maw of some great beast, a passage filled with stalactites descends into the ground. Shapeless swamp extends out in all other directions, some passable, some not.

npcs.name: Bogwalker
npcs.description: putrid depths of the quickmud pits, festering there and becoming warped

868.name: Tangled Thicket
868.description: Several root systems from different trees twine together here, cutting off any further westward passage. Strange calls come from high in the branches, and shadowy forms can be seen darting amongst the thicket of tangled roots. The only passage out is back east, the way you came.

876.name: Deep in the Swamp
876.description: Biting insects swarm around with a dull buzz, and out from some unseen lake the low, haunting call of a loon reaches your ears. The tall, dead grasses hide numerous creatures, and at times you can follow their progress by the curious looking wake formed as they run through the reeds.

877.name: Deep in the Swamp
877.description: Just to your west is an expansive sinkhole, overtop of which is a tangled network of roots, many of which look sturdy enough for you to crawl across. Tall, dead grass blocks your vision in most other directions, but to your south a huge brown tree can be seen towering above the swamp.

878.name: Over a Quagmire
878.description: Several crisscrossing roots from different trees extend over the wide pit of quickmud, avoiding it just like you hope to. The going is slow, and a little risky, but handholds are plentiful among the tangle, and several thick looking logs float below you in the mire. Not much of a safety net, but better than nothing.

879.name: Deep in the Swamp
879.description: The ground here firms up a little, though you can still feel a slight bounce with each step, and few trees or grasses crowd the area. Other than the ever present odor of the quickmud to your south, the place is actually quite pleasant, for a swamp.

880.name: Deep in the Swamp
880.description: The hard leaves of the tall reeds give way to a large sinkhole to your north. A huge uprooted tree, partially submerged in the muck, leads out to a tangle of thick roots spanning the pit. Angry clouds of insects swarm near the quickmud, apparently attracted by the smell, or the possibility of an easy meal trapped in the mire.

881.name: Deep in the Swamp
881.description: Gnarled roots tower over your head, creating twisted cages around the boles of several tall hardwood trees. All manner of tiny creatures scamper amongst the miniature forest of roots, safe from the clutches of most larger predators. Three paths, equidistant from each other, lead out of the small clearing between the trees.

882.name: Shallow Tributary
882.description: A small stream, too shallow for a boat, runs gradually north and eastwards into the swamp from sources unknown. Strange looking creatures slither between the slippery river rounded stones, and tall trees bear in overhead, cloaking the thin channel in twilight. Sskyyth totems mark both banks at the mouth of the creek.

883.name: Shallow Tributary
883.description: Unnatural noises echo out from the banks, and the feeling of eyes upon you is ever harder to ignore as you trudge through the ankle deep water. Now and again dark shapes can be seen flitting through the shadows just ahead or behind, then vanishing into the impenetrable bush on either side.

884.name: Shallow Tributary
884.description: The water rarely rises past your ankles as it burbles over the stones, save for a few deep potholes here and there, where malformed fish flee the jaws of monstrous larvae. Even the trees and vines that choke the banks have a fey look about them, as though the whole place were rotten at its very core.

885.name: Red Water
885.description: A thick algae has bloomed somewhere upstream, washing downwards and diluting as it goes, giving the water here an odd red tinge if the light hits it just so. The tiny red plants stain your boots and leggings, letting off a faint coppery scent as they dry. Just to the north, a break in the foliage reveals a thin trail.

npcs.name: Wild vampire
npcs.description: Apelike arms end in oversized hands with flesh tearing claws, and needle thin teeth fill a gaping maw.  Hateful red eyes bore into you, looking for

886.name: Bloodbloom Lake
886.description: A few dusty old texts mention this hidden lake, and even fewer people, though its effects are seen by many once every several years. A thick red algae grows on the bed of the shallow body of water, thus its name. Some seasons show a particularly vigorous bloom, resulting in the Frektal and much of the Great Ancestral Swamp taking on the same reddish hue.

887.name: Shallow Lakebed
887.description: A carpet of branches and larger trunks covers the bed of the lake, all coated with the thick red algae. Each step stirs up a cloud of the stuff, making your footing treacherous amongst the snags. Thankfully the water is little more than a few feet deep in places, and the largest logs jut well above the surface.

888.name: Western shore of Bloodbloom Lake
888.description: Several trails run off into the brush on the western shore, leading to what looks like a small clearing within the bush. Even ankle deep in the eerie red waters, the darkened forest seems fairly uninviting. The lake follows a wide curve southward, wrapping around the tiny island in the middle.

889.name: Rutted Land
889.description: Deep ruts and grooves score the earth within the tight clearing, especially in and around the roots of trees. Something has spent quite some time nosing through the foul smelling dirt here, but for purposes unknown. A thin trail leads east to the lake, or north between to darkened trees.

npcs.name: Salivating swine creature
npcs.description: form.  Blood and entrails hang from its massive tusks, rage sparkling in

890.name: Secret Bank
890.description: Another small clearing, hidden from view of the lake, allows for quiet surveillance of the entire body of water. The ground feels warm in spots, likely some fell creature was in here watching you not very long ago. Strange sounds echo from the forest all around, and perhaps you are still being spied upon.

891.name: Southern Banks of Bloodbloom Lake
891.description: Twisted snags poke above the water like bloody, grasping hands reaching out at you. Often enough, one below the surface manages a hold on your foot, and sends you for another tumble into the coppery smelling water. Thick, evil looking bush chokes the shores to your west and south.

892.name: Southern Banks of Bloodbloom Lake
892.description: To the north, the inaccessible thicket crowds the small island, while to the south the shallow lake melts into a large dam of rotting tree trunks, and farther back, the forest. The water curves east and north around the islet, vanishing out of sight in the gloom.

893.name: Shallow Lakebed
893.description: Small waves made by your passing lap against the rock strewn shore to the east. Many footprints of indeterminate nature are visible in the mud. Dozens of logs litter the southern banks, piled there as if from some great clearing of land. Where such a large open space could be hidden is not, however, apparent.

894.name: Stony Shore
894.description: Chunks of hard red stone cover the shoreline, many of which appear to be pieces of large blocks, tooled smooth and cut down to size. Several huge boulders remain untouched, save by the encroaching swamp, wrapping its roots and vines around them. A barely visible trail leads into the foreboding brush.

npcs.name: Jigante
npcs.description: towers over you.  He looks human, ish.  Mayhaps a feral child; which would account for his madness, perhaps even retardation, but not for his immense

895.name: Stony Shore
895.description: Chips of red stone, most smaller than your hand, litter the shore all around you. Their hue is unlike any other rock found in the swamp, but the tool marks upon them are obvious, so they must have been carried in over some great distance - no small feat given the soft ground. More stones, larger than these, cover the banks to the south.

900.name: Curious Islet
900.description: What looked like a dense thicket from the outside was actually just a wall of brush encircling the island. Here within is a ring of jagged red stones, like a maw of bloody, broken teeth. All around are scattered the bones of those unfortunate enough to become trapped here. A gnarled plant with twisted roots grows here, its leaves dark and waxy.

npcs.name: Tectonic entity
npcs.description: animating it into a swirling humanoid form at your approach.

814.name: Winding Up the Tree
814.description: You scale the notches that are cut into the bark of the tree, the effort of climbing combined with the humidity dampening your brow with sweat. Your hands are getting filthy from the support lines and the mosses that coat the bark. The tree is so large that its surface supports a tiny ecosystem of its own, speckled with lichens and fungi, under which scurry a myriad of insects.

d.to_room_name: At the Base of the Tree

766.name: In the Swamp
766.description: Lifeless patches of quicksand extend into the murk and passage east deeper into the swamp is cut off from here. You can make out the faint buzzing of dragonflies. The surface of stone cylinder remains featureless on its east side. Northwest or southwest, your only choice is to circle back around it.

npcs.name: Mosquito
npcs.description: drain a holiim dry in a minute flat.

813.name: Along a Wooden Walkway
813.description: This platform is rather narrow, closely hugging the trunk of a swamp tree. A series of notches have been cut into the living wood to act as a crude set of stairs, which lead down the tree towards the ground. You can smell the stagnant water of the swamp keenly (a little too keenly) and hang onto a vine when the aroma makes you a little dizzy.

e.to_zone: Sskyth Treetop Village - Mt. M'chel Region
